prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Spawn one subagent to generate a second briefing from the same evidence surface.

risks_fired.evidence: Spawn one subagent to generate a second briefing
risks_fired.fix_hint: Direct imperative + subagent architecture absorbs preamble cost; design is correct.

risks_fired.evidence: Write a one-page briefing on the same challenge to `module-5/briefing-second-run.md`.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Fresh file path mitigates; Claude will not hesitate on a new destination.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Compare the first and second briefing.

risks_fired.evidence: Self-consistency issues: claims that are both unsupported or partly grounded AND unstable across runs.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Claude reads two files as external artifacts, not its own work; charity risk is structurally mitigated by the comparison task design.

risks_fired.evidence: Whether the winning groundedness judge should change. Two runs is too few to chase noise; only call for a change if you can name a specific failure class the current judge missed.
risks_fired.fix_hint: The evidence-based quality bar (name a specific failure class) forces reasoned reporting; softening risk is low.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I have output I want to quality-control against fabrication. Build me a judge pr

risks_fired.evidence: Pick at least one of the four to drop or rewrite for the failure modes specific to my output — don't keep the list as-is.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add: "In one sentence, explain why you dropped or rewrote that item." Forces genuine reasoning over minimum-effort compliance.
